LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RAYMOND YOUNG, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA RAYMOND YOUNG LIVE POULTRY, AKA RAYMOND YOUNG POULTRY'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - PLAINTIFF'S PETITION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING. PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE THE BREACH OF CONTRACT OR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF'S CITATION TO PARAGRAPH 27 OF THE RULES IS MISPLACED. THAT PARAGRAPH PERTAINS TO ENFORCEMENT OF MARKET RULES AND DISCIPLINE. THIS CASE INVOLVES A POLICY CHANGE NOT DISCIPLINE. PLAINTIFF ALSO DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY EXPLAIN THE REASON FOR A DELAY IN SEEKING RELIEF AT THIS TIME. GRANTING THE INJUNCTION WOULD ACTUALLY CHANGE, NOT PRESERVE, THE STATUS QUO. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

Off Calendar Dec-08-2011 Set For Trial On Sep-24-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

An Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction (Per Ex Parte Order Filed On 08-02-11)
Off Calendar Sep-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF RAYMOND YOUNG, individually and dba RAYMOND YOUNG LIVE POULTRY, aka RAYMOND YOUNG POULTRY'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION.

APPEARANCES: William M. Balin, Esq. of Balin & Kotler, LLP 241-7360 for pltf & moving party; Eve H. Cervantez, Esq. of Altshuler Berzon LLP 421-7151 for defts & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - PLAINTIFF'S PETITION FOR A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO DEMONSTRATE A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING. PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE THE BREACH OF CONTRACT OR PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL. PLAINTIFF'S CITATION TO PARAGRAPH 27 OF THE RULES IS MISPLACED. THAT PARAGRAPH PERTAINS TO ENFORCEMENT OF MARKET RULES AND DISCIPLINE. THIS CASE INVOLVES A POLICY CHANGE NOT DISCIPLINE. PLAINTIFF ALSO DOES NOT SUFFICIENTLY EXPLAIN THE REASON FOR A DELAY IN SEEKING RELIEF AT THIS TIME. GRANTING THE INJUNCTION WOULD ACTUALLY CHANGE, NOT PRESERVE, THE STATUS QUO. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
